mapping.a_element: Monte Carlo resampling of observed data to approximate a statistic's sampling distribution (bootstrap)
mapping.b_element: Monte Carlo Markov Chain sampling of the parameter posterior
95% C.L. upper limit on the interaction strength

brief_justification: The overlap is only the generic label 'Monte Carlo uncertainty quantification producing a 95% statement.' The actual machinery differs: paper A resamples observed time-series data (block/residual/sieve/wild bootstrap) and applies adaptive conformal calibration to guarantee prediction-interval coverage under dependence, whereas paper B runs forward hydrodynamical simulations, trains an emulator surrogate, and uses MCMC posterior inference to place a parameter bound — a simulation-based Bayesian inference pipeline with no bootstrap/conformal-calibration role to map onto.
